Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search Slack for messages about Heartland and Swift rate increases using conversations_search_messages with queries like "Heartland rate" and "Swift rate" (or similar). Finds Elena's directive in C004 (#executive) ordering a vendor strategy memo by Wednesday, Marcus owning it. Finds Alejandro's analysis post confirming Heartland +20% effective Q3 and Swift +15% immediate plus $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment. Finds Marcus's finance thread about the unresolved Heartland Q1 invoice being used as leverage. Read action.
Search emails for the vendor rate increase formal notices using search_emails with queries like "rate increase" or "Heartland" or "Swift" or "Formal Notice" (or similar). Finds Jake Loomis email (Heartland) announcing 20% rate increase across all service tiers effective July 1, 2026, tying it to the unresolved Q1 dispute. Finds Greg Pallone email (Swift) announcing 15% base rate increase plus $500 DOT surcharge effective immediately April 11. Read action.
Search QuickBooks for all outstanding bills using quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll true. Finds 17 bills including Heartland Q1 invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 ($12,800, payment held, $4,800 disputed), Heartland Abdi move HM-2026-0418 ($2,150), Swift bills ($11,010 across Tran, Venkatesh, Kovac), Mosaic damage accrual ($90,000), DOT penalty accrual ($50,000), KeyMove piano claim ($1,200), AWS ($42,870). Read action.
Search QuickBooks for all invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices to understand what clients owe and outstanding AR across all accounts. Read action.
Search emails for NorthWind and the client ultimatum using search_emails with queries like "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "PathFinder" (or similar). Finds Pam Kowalski's formal email to david.chen@moveops.com (CC victor.huang@northwindtech.com) stating NorthWind is evaluating whether MoveOps can continue, citing Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach, Emilia Cruz piano damage, and the missed April 14 recovery plan. She mentions preliminary conversations with PathFinder Logistics. Read action.
Search Slack for NorthWind discussions using conversations_search_messages with queries like "NorthWind" or "Pam" or "save-or-lose" (or similar). Finds David Chen's post in C004 (#executive) on April 26 at 02:40 confirming the formal escalation from Pam, that she bypassed Catalina, and PathFinder conversations are active. Finds Elena's earlier April 16 reply in the same channel: "Handle it within the team. No account reshuffle right now." Read action.
Search Slack for Catalina-related messages using conversations_search_messages with queries like "Catalina" (or similar). Finds Marcus's post noting "customer-engagement exposure comments from Mina and Emeka, with Catalina still missing." Finds David's message in #customer-engagement about NorthWind being save-or-lose and Catalina's April 14 recovery plan promise never delivered. Finds Catalina's own message admitting she is overwhelmed. Read action.
Search emails for Catalina communications using search_emails with query "Catalina" (or similar). Finds Catalina's April 14 email to Pam promising a recovery plan within the week. Finds the Terraform reply-all incident where Catalina accidentally sent internal pricing to rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com. Read action.
Search Slack for Chloe's capacity messages using conversations_search_messages with queries like "capacity May" or "coordinator hire" (or similar). Finds Chloe's post in C006 (#operations): "Need leadership eyes on May capacity now. We are already functionally at max coordinator load." Finds David's post in C004 requesting a temporary coordinator for 4-6 weeks. Finds Chloe's end-of-day message: "End of day and still no answer from Elena on temp coverage. This is not sustainable." Read action.
Search CRM for all at-risk deals using crm_search_deals with queries for "NorthWind", "Vectral", "Mosaic", "BrightLoop", "StormCloud", "Terraform", "Canopy", "Sunbelt", "Palmetto" (or similar). Finds NorthWind Denver expansion deal ($14,700), Vectral Q3 renewal deal (at_risk stage), Mosaic deals including the Okoye damage risk deal ($2,700), Terraform first batch deal ($7,500), and others. Read action.
Search CRM for company records using crm_search_companies for each client to get account context and relationship details. Read action.
Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records on the Relocations table (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) to get May volume picture and coordinator assignments. Finds 63 total records, multiple In Progress moves across all clients. Read action.
Search Linear for existing tickets using linear_list_issues to identify stale or misprioritized tickets and untracked risks. Finds vendor memo ticket (linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36) assigned to Marcus, NorthWind retention ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1), Heartland dispute consolidation ticket (linear_issue_6f33a369d77e, overdue), margin dataset ticket (linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170, overdue). Read action.
Search Slack for Sunbelt and Palmetto issues using conversations_search_messages with queries like "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice swap" (or similar). Finds David's post treating the swapped invoice PDFs as a second pricing-exposure incident. Finds the chain of failures: Emeka flagged overload, Hana rushed the batch, engineering had a dormant attachment-safety ticket. Read action.
Search emails and Slack for StormCloud Analytics using search_emails and conversations_search_messages with queries like "StormCloud" or "Liam Park" or "Jae-won" (or similar). Finds Liam Park's escalation email citing Jae-won Kim's unsigned lease, Simone Richter's frozen stipend, and the Ridgeline Robotics referral that was never followed up. Read action.
Look up email addresses for Marcus Thorne, Chloe Vance, David Chen, and Elena Rostova using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts. Finds marcus.thorne@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com, david.chen@moveops.com, elena.rostova@moveops.com. Read action.
Update the vendor strategy memo Linear ticket (linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36) using linear_update_issue to set the deadline to May 2, 2026 and add context about Elena's escalation. Write action.
Update the NorthWind retention ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1) using linear_update_issue to reflect CEO-level visibility, Pam's formal ultimatum, PathFinder competitor conversations, and the urgency of a retention response. Write action.
Create Linear tickets using linear_create_issue on the Operations team for untracked risks: Vectral Q3 renewal churn risk (P1), May coordinator capacity and hire decision (P1), Catalina account coverage gap (P1), Swift rate increase margin impact (P2), and any other risks found without existing tickets. Write action.
Update CRM engagement notes using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: NOTE) on NorthWind (company_northwind) with details on Pam's ultimatum, PathFinder conversations, chain-of-custody breach, piano damage, and missed recovery plan. Write action.
Update CRM engagement notes using crm_create_engagement on Vectral (company_vectral) with the renewal risk status, 15% discount demand, 10% counter-offer, and vendor rate increase impact on margins. Write action.
Update CRM engagement notes using crm_create_engagement on Mosaic (company_mosaic) with the $90K damage claim, $40K insurance gap, DOT compliance exposure on the Tanaka shipment, and account relationship risk. Write action.
Update CRM engagement notes on any other accounts where the vendor situation or open incidents threaten the relationship, such as BrightLoop (April failures plus May expansion), StormCloud (multi-failure recovery), Sunbelt/Palmetto (invoice swap pricing exposure). Write action.
Send email using send_email from elena.rostova@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com with subject about the vendor memo deadline. Body must state the new hard deadline is May 2, reference the April 29 meeting, and specify what the memo must cover. Write action.
Send email using send_email from elena.rostova@moveops.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com with subject about May capacity. Body must ask for her capacity numbers for May and whether she needs the additional coordinator headcount approved. Write action.
Create a calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event for April 29, 2026, 45 minutes, titled "Q2 Vendor Strategy and Risk Review" with attendees marcus.thorne@moveops.com, david.chen@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com, and elena.rostova@moveops.com. Write action.
Post a summary message in the #executive Slack channel (C004) using conversations_add_message covering the full situation: financial exposure, vendor rate increases, at-risk accounts, May capacity, and Catalina situation, so Priya has visibility. Write action.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
